name: MI6 Diana Theory
description: The MI6 Diana Theory posits that the death of Diana, Princess of Wales, in 1997 was not accidental but a planned assassination orchestrated by the British Secret Intelligence Service (MI6), allegedly acting on behalf of the British establishment or members of the Royal Family. Proponents believe Diana was becoming a liability due to her divorce from Prince Charles, her outspokenness, and her relationship with Dodi Fayed. The theory suggests that MI6 deliberately engineered the car crash in Paris to eliminate her. The theory gained traction shortly after Diana's death, fueled by public distrust and conspiracy narratives. Key proponents often cite alleged inconsistencies in the official investigation, witness testimonies, and purported leaked documents as evidence. Mainstream perspectives, including the official French investigation and the British Operation Paget inquiry, concluded that the crash was caused by the reckless actions of the driver, Henri Paul, who was under the influence of alcohol and prescription drugs, combined with the aggressive pursuit by paparazzi. These investigations found no credible evidence of MI6 involvement or a deliberate plot. Criticisms of the MI6 theory highlight the lack of concrete proof, reliance on speculation, and the tendency to misinterpret or selectively use available information. The theory continues to circulate, fueled by documentaries, books, and online forums, despite being widely dismissed by official sources and mainstream media.
subtitle: Alleged assassination by British Intelligence.

claims.claim: MI6 deliberately caused the car crash.
claims.evidence: Inconsistencies in the crash scene, alleged tampering with the Mercedes, and eyewitness accounts claiming the presence of a white Fiat Uno.
claims.criticism: Official investigations found no evidence of foul play, attributing the crash to driver negligence and paparazzi pursuit. The 'white Fiat Uno' was investigated and deemed not directly involved in causing the crash.

claims.claim: Diana was pregnant with Dodi Fayed's child and planned to marry him, posing a threat to the monarchy.
claims.evidence: Claims from Mohamed Al-Fayed and others who knew Diana, asserting her intentions to marry Dodi.
claims.criticism: Medical examinations after Diana's death found no evidence of pregnancy. Her close friends and associates have disputed the marriage claims, suggesting it was unlikely.

claims.claim: The driver, Henri Paul, was an MI6 asset who was either complicit in the plot or manipulated into causing the crash.
claims.evidence: Allegations that Henri Paul had connections to intelligence agencies and that his blood samples were tampered with.
claims.criticism: Official investigations confirmed that Henri Paul was heavily intoxicated and driving at excessive speed. Claims of intelligence connections and blood sample tampering have been thoroughly investigated and found to be unsubstantiated.

keyFigures.name: Mohamed Al-Fayed
keyFigures.role: Prominent proponent who funded investigations and promoted the assassination theory.

keyFigures.name: Lord Stevens
keyFigures.role: Head of Operation Paget, the official British inquiry into Diana's death, which concluded there was no evidence of a conspiracy.

mythologyConnections: Themes of betrayal and power within royal families

timeline.event: Diana, Princess of Wales, dies in a car crash in Paris.
Operation Paget report is published, concluding there was no evidence of conspiracy.
Continued proliferation of MI6 assassination theories in various media.

sources.author: Operation Paget
sources.work: Report of the Inquiry into the Deaths of Diana, Princess of Wales, and Mr. Dodi Al Fayed
sources.title: Operation Paget
